title: About
text:
This site tracks legislation in the Iowa 91st General Assembly (2025–2026), covering the Iowa House and Senate. It provides browsable pages for individual bills and legislators, including vote records, bill histories, sponsor information, and legislator voting summaries.
The site is built as a static website using Quarto and R, and is hosted on GitHub Pages.

title: About
text:
This site tracks legislation in the Iowa 91st General Assembly (2025–2026), covering the Iowa House and Senate. It provides browsable pages for individual bills and legislators, including vote records, bill histories, sponsor information, and legislator voting summaries.
The site is built as a static website using Quarto and R, and is hosted on GitHub Pages.

title: About
text:
Data Sources
All legislative data is sourced from LegiScan, a nonpartisan legislative tracking service that aggregates data from state legislatures across the United States. LegiScan provides:

Bill metadata — bill numbers, titles, subjects, and current status
Bill history — a record of actions taken on each bill (introductions, committee referrals, floor votes, etc.)
Roll call votes — individual legislator votes on floor amendments and final passage
Legislator profiles — party affiliation, district, and contact information
Sponsor relationships — which legislators sponsored or co-sponsored each bill

LegiScan data is accessed via their API. Raw data is stored locally and processed into summaries before the site is rendered.

title: About
text:
How Often Data Is Updated
The data pipeline runs automatically every day at midnight CT via a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow. Each run:

Fetches updated data from the LegiScan API
Processes votes and legislator statistics
Re-renders any bill or legislator pages that have changed
Publishes the updated site to GitHub Pages

Data was last fetched on April 04, 2026 at 06:46 AM UTC.

title: About
text:
Attribution

Legislative data: LegiScan (terms of use)
Site framework: Quarto
Hosting: GitHub Pages

This site is an independent project and is not affiliated with the Iowa Legislature or any government agency. Legislative information should be verified against official sources at legis.iowa.gov.
